observations.recommendation:
Recommendation/s:
We recommended to the Municipal Mayor to address the issues brought herein and to:
Existence of unreconciled balance between General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office
a. Direct the Inventory Committee/GSO/Property Unit and the Accounting Unit to identify the details of the variance through the List of PPEs Found at Station (Annex B of the Circular) and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) prepared after the physical inventory to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts as part of the one-time cleansing;
b. Direct the Inventory Committee, Property Unit/GSO and Accounting Unit to reconcile all records and reports pertaining to PPE after the one-time cleansing and establish the correct balances of the PPE account to be recorded in the books of accounts; and
c. Direct the same to review all documents and reports before submission to the Office of the Audit Team to ensure that the details presented thereon are accurate and complete.
Presence of bulk acquisitions without specifying quantities in Property Cards
a. Direct the Property Unit/GSO and Accounting Unit to exhaust available records and reports in order to trace the specific number of items/quantities on the recorded properties acquired in bulk/by set; and
b. Direct the Accounting Unit to ensure that the same meets the capitalization threshold, otherwise, charged the same to expense or Prior Period Adjustment.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accounting to revisit the classification and presentation of prior period adjustments in accordance with Paragraph 118 of IPSAS 1, which require that prior period errors be presented separately in the SCNAE and be disclosed adequately in the Notes to Financial Statements.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure the consistent recognition of revenue from fund transfers related to completed projects under the appropriate account, based on the nature of the transaction, in order to uphold the qualitative characteristics of financial reporting, particularly comparability and understandability, as prescribed in the IPSAS Conceptual Framework.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accounting Office to ensure that similar transactions in the future are properly classified and disclosed in full compliance with applicable International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS).
Specifically, require the restatement of the comparative amounts of the accounts affected by the identified prior period error in accordance with Paragraphs 51 to 53 of IPSAS 3. Additionally, the required disclosures on the nature, amount, and impact of the prior period error should be provided in the Notes to Financial Statements for the succeeding year, as prescribed under Paragraph 54 of IPSAS 3.
Henceforth, all future corrections of prior period errors must be appropriately accounted for as adjustments to the opening balance of accumulated surplus/deficit (equity) of the earliest comparative period presented, and should not be routed through the current year’s surplus or deficit, in accordance with IPSAS requirements.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:
a. Direct the Accounting Unit to establish the correct balances of the Inventory and Expense account to be recorded in the books through proper adjusting entry, taking into consideration the proper treatment of Prior Period Adjustment;
b. Establish controls and accountability for semi-expendable properties –prepare and maintain Registry of Semi-Expendable Property Issued (RegSPI) and ensure that Semi-Expendable Property Card (SPC), and Semi-Expendable Property Ledger Card (SPLC) are continuously and appropriately maintained, as well as other prescribed forms under COA Circular No. 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022 as maybe applicable such as Inventory Transfer Report (ITR), Receipt of Returned Semi-Expendable Property (RRSP), and Report of Semi-Expendable Property Issued (RSPI); and
c. Assign accountable personnel/office for inventory management to ensure ongoing compliance, accurate tracking, and proper valuation of semi-expendable properties in subsequent reporting period.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accounting Office and the Municipal Budget Office to submit a detailed breakdown of the adjustments reflected in the reconciliation between the actual amounts presented in the Statement of Comparison of Budget and Actual Amounts (SCBAA) for the year ended December 31, 2024, for verification. A justification for the noted deficiencies should likewise be submitted.
Henceforth, management should ensure that future submissions of the SCBAA and its corresponding reconciliation include detailed disclosures and a breakdown of material items in the Notes to the Financial Statements to enhance the fair presentation and transparency of the financial reports.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accounting Office to ensure strict adherence to the prescribed account classification under Annex B of COA Circular No. 2015-009. In particular, payments related to foreign travel, regardless of how they are labeled (e.g., registration fees), should be evaluated based on their substance to ensure they are charged to the appropriate account, such as Travelling Expenses – Foreign.

Recommendation:
We recommended the Municipal Mayor to require the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Health Officer to submit the required supporting documents to facilitate the review of the herein transactions.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accounting Office to submit the complete supporting documents for the donations of Property, Plant, and Equipment to the various barangays, including those pertaining to Barangay Dicklum, to ensure the validity and sufficiency of the recorded transactions.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer to provide a written justification, with supporting computations, if necessary, to resolve this issue. If not properly addressed, the payees should be required to refund the amount.
Henceforth, ensure that all disbursements are strictly charged to the correct appropriation items, based on the actual nature of the expenditure.

Recommendation/s:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to:
a.  Promptly retrieve all bank statements from the concerned bank branches;
b. Prepare and submit the required Bank Reconciliation Statements in accordance with Paragraphs 3.4 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

1. Obtain assurance that the recorded project costs in the CIP accounts are complete, before its reclassification to the appropriate PPE accounts to ensure accurate accounting of the actual cost of the assets.  
2. The Municipal Engineering Office shall provide a Certificate of Completion, Statement of Work Accomplished and Inspection Report showing 100% completion as supporting documents to the JEV.

observations.recommendation:
With each of the following conditions, we recommended to the management to:
Non-conduct of complete physical count of its PPE for CY 2023
1) Monitor that the Inventory Committee has adequate number of members and representatives from the Accounting and Property Division/Unit for the sole purpose of the one-time cleansing of the PPE accounts of the LGU;
2) temporarily relieve the members of the Inventory Committee of all their regular duties in order to devote their full time in the conduct of the physical inventory taking until completed;
3) direct the GSO/Property Unit to record the physical count daily using the ICF per PPE sub-major account group which will be the basis in the preparation of RPCPPE after the physical count;
4) direct the Inventory Committee/GSO/Property Unit to prepare a List of PPEs Found at Station (Annex B of the Circular) and List of Non-Existing/Missing PPEs (Annex C of the Circular) after the physical inventory and submit these lists to the Accounting Unit for recording in the books of accounts of the LGU to establish the existence and proper accountability of the PPE accounts; and
5) Ensure that the guidelines and procedures in the conduct of physical count of PPE as provided in COA Circular 2020-006 are strictly observed to facilitate the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances.
Existence of unreconciled balance between General Services Office and the Municipal Accounting Office
Direct the Inventory Committee, Property Unit/GSO and Accounting Unit to reconcile all records and reports pertaining to PPE after the one-time cleansing and establish the correct balances of the PPE account to be recorded in the books of accounts; and
direct the same to review all documents and reports before submission to the Office of the Audit Team to ensure that the details presented thereon are accurate and complete.
Presence of bulk acquisitions without specifying quantities in Property Cards
1) Direct the Property Unit/GSO and Accounting Unit to exhaust available records and reports in order to trace the specific number of items/quantities on the recorded properties acquired in bulk/by set; and
2) direct the Accounting Unit to ensure that the same meets the capitalization threshold, otherwise, charged the same to expense or Prior Period Adjustment.
Capitalization of costs of Repairs and Maintenance; and
1) Direct the Municipal Accountant to drop in the PPE accounts the reported items which did not qualify as PPE (but of Repairs and Maintenance) through proper journal entry. Hence, ensure that all property and equipment recorded in the books qualify as PPE pursuant to Item 4.2 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 and COA Circular 2022-004 dated May 31, 2022.
Not completely adopting the Uniform Property Identification System for PPE
1) Direct the Property Unit/GSO to ensure the assignment of a unique Property Number for each of the PPE items of all PPE accounts of the LGU following the required the numbering system as provided in Section 5 of COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020; and
2) instruct the Property Unit/GSO to ensure that Property Stickers attached to PPE items show all vital information of the PPE as required under section 5.7 of the Circular.

We recommended to the Municipal Accounting Office to draw a journal entry voucher reclassifying the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) to its appropriate account, if warranted, and furnish the Audit Team a copy for reference We also recommended to the Municipal Accounting Office to thoroughly review the nature of all other items under the Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) and if found to be improperly classified, reclassify the same to proper accounts supported with complete working paper and furnish a copy to the Audit Team.

Auditor’s Rejoinder:

“While the procuring entity’s representative/Project Engineer may allow the immediate start of work under any Change Order or Extra Work Order, it is clearly required under Section 3.2 Annex “E” of the IRR of RA 9184 that the same must still be subject to availability of funds before undertaking any additional work. Thus, a valid appropriation relative thereto is a prerequisite before a Local Government Unit can incur obligation.”

We recommended to the management the following procedures prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 on the one-time cleansing of PPE accounts:
1. For lost PPEs with identified accountable personnel 
a. Issue demand letter to concerned accountable officer/personnel demanding the production of the PPE he/she is accountable for; and
b. The Accounting Unit shall take up the necessary accounting entries to recognize in the books of accounts the loss of PPE and to set up the corresponding receivables from concerned accountable officers/personnel for the non-existing/missing PPEs that could not be produced upon demand. 

 2. For non-existing/missing PPEs for which accountability could not be established
a. The Head of the Agency shall cause the conduct of an investigation to determine the last known location/office where the missing PPE items were issued/installed, person accountable, circumstances of the loss, and the persons responsible for the loss, among others; 
b. If accountability/responsibility over the missing PPE was pinpointed after investigation, demand shall be made from the accountable personnel to produce the item or pay the depreciated replacement cost, thereof;
c. The Accounting Unit shall be furnished the certified copy of the investigation report and demand letter to be used as basis to recognize loss of PPE and to set up the corresponding receivables from the concerned accountable officer/personnel; and 
d. If accountability/responsibility over the missing PPE could not be pinpointed after investigation, authority for derecognition thereof from the books of accounts may be requested from the COA. Pending the grant of authority by the COA, the said PPEs shall remain in the books of accounts

We recommended to the management to establish a comprehensive control system as soon as possible, to ensure efficient and effective operations of its economic enterprise, particularly the Heavy Equipment, which may include the following:
Require a Job 
Order/Request from the implementing department (i.e. MEO) detailing the schedule of equipment works to be done on a per project basis with details on the date of usage, specific equipment required and the expected output.
Attach to the Job 
Order is the OBRE/Certificate of availability of funds to ensure funds for the obligations to be incurred.
Ensure that all data 
required in the utilization slips are completely filled up, so that the cost per usage can be determined immediately for proper matching of income with expense.
The utilization slips 
shall be signed as “validated as to the number of hours rendered” by the Barangay Officials where the project was undertaken or by the MEO officials in- charge of the project.
Inspection Report 
and Accomplishment Reports prepared and certified by authorized officials shall be required for every payment of HE rental.
The management should also address the issue on the monitoring process during the duration of the projects/ activities to ensure that all Heavy Equipment are efficiently used for its specific purpose.

We recommended to the management to stop the practice of granting cash advance to the Disbursing Officer (DO) for the travels of other officials and employees. Instead, issue a check directly in the name of the travelling officials and employees as his/her cash advance subject to liquidation. Otherwise, if formed part of the DO’s liquidation, it must be supported with complete traveling documents by the concerned employee. 
Moreover, increase the fidelity bond of the SDO/HoA for confidential fund or limit the cash advance up to the maximum accountability only.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to record the receivables and income from HE rental duly supported with the certified list of collectibles from the Heavy Equipment Office (EE), the utilization slips duly validated and certified by the end user/ requesting office/barangay. Institute action to enforce collections especially from the Barangays. Establish a control system in the Heavy Equipment Operations so that assurance for availability of appropriations and funds are secured first prior to equipment usage in order to maximize its profitability and avoid future uncollectible accounts. For the uncollected rental from LBP, recognize the receivable, income and payable accounts supported with a signed Lease Contract Agreement and billing statement of the uncollected monthly rental.

In addition, proper revenue recognition shall be observed as stated in IPSAS 9 and PD1445 and the accrual method of accounting shall be consistently applied in all books of accounts in compliance to the International Public Sector Accounting Standards and to present fairly the accounts of the LGU in the financial statements as at year end.

Recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Accounting Office should make the necessary Journal Entries to record the receivables from delinquent tax payers at the end of CY 2019, supported by a list duly certified by the Municipal Treasurer’s Office. In addition, at the beginning of the subsequent year, the Municipal Treasurer’s Office shall ensure that the amount of RPT/SET collectibles provided to the Municipal Accounting Office is accurate and complete in accordance with Section 20 of the Manual on the New Government Accounting System (NGAS). Reconciliation of the RPT/SET receivable accounts shall be made periodically, to enable the fair presentation of the account balances.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the Municipal Accountant to take recognition of the events after reporting date that necessitates adjustments of the amounts recognized in the financial statements for fair presentation of the financial statements as at year-end. In the subsequent years, constant follow up of the submitted liquidations in relation to the cash advance for confidential expenses shall be made for timely recording of its liquidation.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to establish a comprehensive control system as soon as possible, to ensure efficient and effective operations of its economic enterprise, particularly the Heavy Equipment, which may include the following:
Require a Job Order/Request from the implementing department (i.e. MEO) detailing the schedule of equipment works to be done on a per project basis with details on the date of usage, specific equipment required and the expected output.
Attach to the Job Order is the OBRE/Certificate of availability of funds to ensure funds for the obligations to be incurred.
Ensure that all data required in the utilization slips are completely filled up, so that the cost per usage can be determined immediately for proper matching of income with expense.
The utilization slips shall be signed as “validated as to the number of hours rendered” by the Barangay Officials where the project was undertaken or by the MEO officials in- charge of the project.
Inspection Report and Accomplishment Reports prepared and certified by authorized officials shall be required for every payment of HE rental.
The management should also address the issue on the monitoring process during the duration of the projects/ activities to ensure that all Heavy Equipment are efficiently used for its specific purpose.

Recommendation:
We recommended to the management to stop the practice of granting cash advance to the Disbursing Officer (DO) for the travels of other officials and employees. Instead, issue a check directly in the name of the travelling officials and employees as his/her cash advance subject to liquidation. Otherwise, if formed part of the DO’s liquidation, it must be supported with complete traveling documents by the concerned employee.
Moreover, increase the fidelity bond of the SDO/HoA for confidential fund or limit the cash advance up to the maximum accountability only.

Recommendation:

Submit all the documents required in Section 9.1.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 for all approved variation orders and approved time extensions granted to all the nine (9) projects mentioned above, which shall include the following:
Copy of the LGU’s report establishing the necessity/ justification for the need of variation order, which includes the computation as to the quantities of the additional work involved per item (if there is any), the date of inspection conducted and its results and a detailed estimate of the unit cost of such item of work for new unit costs;
Copy of the Revised PERT/CPM Network Diagram reflecting the effect of the additional time on the contract period together with its corresponding detailed computations.
Report of the LGU on the validation procedures conducted to establish the need for time extensions, computation showing in sufficient detail how the number of days of extension was determined.
Certified reports from proper authorities to establish existence of the grounds for extension such as the PAG-ASA Report covering the area where the project is located for reasons due to bad weather condition;

Require the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly check and review the completeness of the supporting documents in accordance with Section 9.1.1.2 of COA Circular No. 2012-001 before claims for progress billing are processed for payment.

We recommended that Management:

1)	Enforce collection from D’ Limitless by exhausting all remedies to collect them since the unpaid amount of P1,052,241.54 represents payments made in excess of the contractual obligations of the LGU.
2)	Follow up the legal actions taken on LT Gersan to compensate for its receivables and make available the funds for other priority projects.
3)	Conduct regular review of the receivable accounts and ensure that only valid receivables are recorded in the books.
4)	Analyze receivable accounts which are dormant for a period of 10 years or more and take necessary actions to support the dropping off in the books of accounts by requesting for its Write Off pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.
5)	Retrieve copies of the MOA between the LGU and the NGOs/POs to serve as a basis in enforcing liquidation/ payment of funds transferred to existing and capable NGOs/POs,
observations.managementAction: Collection was made on 1 of the debtors in CY 2018.
observations.reason: Difficulty in retrieving the documents of those long outstanding receivables.

observations.recommendation:
We recommend that management fast track the implementation of the above projects so that the purpose for which the funds were granted is achieved, thus benefitting the constituents.  

Require the Municipal Engineer to conduct quick resolutions to address the lack of water source for its water system projects, identify other possible sources to commence its implementation.  In the future undertaking, conduct feasibility study first, before the implementation of the project, to determine if the project is feasible or not.

Require the Bids and Awards Committee to start immediately the procurement process for the ADM water system project. Device a system to properly handle and control the flow of the documents to avoid loss and pinpoint the person accountable for its loss. Over all, ensure that funds from other government agencies are implemented on a timely manner and make sure that problems encountered are addressed immediately by concerned personnel for effective and efficient implementation of the projects.

Require the General Services Division Officer (GSDO) to declare and insure all the government buildings and heavy equipment in line with the Property Insurance Law paying the total annual premium due every first quarter of the ensuing year; The GSDO should prepare an updated inventory report of these properties congruent to the Municipal Accounting Office inventory records to ensure that they are totally complete and are all fully-safeguarded. Set an adequate budget to cover the cost of insurance coverage.

Instruct the Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant to devise an effective monitoring system regarding the prevention of double-taxation among regular licensees; Ensure that those municipal tax withheld in advance are credited from the Total Assessed Business Tax upon renewal of business permit every January of the succeeding year; The Municipal Accountant should issue a certification of municipal tax withheld bearing the necessary details about the transaction which may be presented by the concerned licensee upon renewal of the business permit to the Business and Licensing Office which will then serve as a concrete proof in crediting the business tax withheld; Encourage the regular licensees to submit a sworn statement of gross receipts or sales with complete listing of all of their sales transactions with a separate table for those business engagements involving the Municipality of Manolo Fortich only, if there are any, for easy identification, review, verification and cross-reference of the Business and Licensing Office with the municipal tax or business tax collected in advance.

We recommended that management instruct the Bids and Awards Committee (BAC) to discontinue this practice unless the procurement is genuinely emergency in nature agreeable to the abovementioned provisions; Adhere to the Government Procurement Law closely and faithfully in all procurement activities of the Local Government Unit.

We recommended that the LGU refrain from entering into contracts which are disadvantageous to the government and strictly observe pertinent laws and regulations to safeguard the interest of the government at all times.

We recommended management, through the Bids and Awards Committee and the Municipal Accountant, to ensure completeness of the supporting documents to the disbursement vouchers relative to the conduct of public bidding from the procurement planning stage up to the issuance of the Notice to Proceed.
observations.reason:
There were still transactions with deficient documentations.

Lack of manpower to scrutinize all procurement documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended management:

	Ensure compliance to the stipulations in the Memorandum of Agreements entered into by the LGU to avoid unauthorized deviations.
	Scrutinize Memorandum of Agreements entered into by the LGU to include responsibilities of all the parties thereto, to ensure transparency and proper accountability for the funds granted.
	Ensure completeness of the supporting documents to the Disbursement Voucher in the release of funds to POs as required in Section 2 of COA Circular 2012-001. 
	Take up receivable for the amount released by debiting the account “Due from NGO/PO” and require liquidation from the NGO/PO.
	Require financial and physical status reports together with all the supporting documents in the utilization of funds from Cristan Villa Neighborhood Rice Trader Association if the period of project implementation has already lapsed based on the Notice to Proceed issued by DOLE.
